title: Personal Data Pseudonymisation Absent
description: Processing personal data without pseudonymisation where it is technically feasible conflicts with GDPR Article 25(1) (privacy by design) and Article 5(1)(e) (storage limitation).

finding.message: Personal data used without pseudonymisation at '{{token}}' in {{file}} at line {{line}}.

references: Replace direct identifiers with pseudonymous tokens in analytics and monitoring systems.
Use a token vault to map pseudonymous IDs to real identifiers, with strict access controls.
Hash or tokenize user IDs in logs, analytics events, and telemetry payloads.
GDPR Recital 78 emphasises pseudonymisation as a key privacy-by-design technique.
